children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
In terms of Sections 20, 21 and 21A of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, the Reserve
Bank of India (hereinafter referred to as 'RBI' or 'Reserve Bank') acts as banker to the
Central and State Governments. RBI carries out the general banking business of the
Central and State Governments through its own offices and through the offices of the
Agency Banks appointed under Section 45 of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, by
mutual Agreement, having regard to public interest and convenience of banking
development. The Reserve Bank, in its role as banker to the Central Government and State
Governments, hereby issues the following instructions/directions, to the Agency Banks, for
the seamless conduct of Government business.

children.title: A. Short Title and Commencement
children.text: A. Short Title and Commencement

children.title: 1. These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India [Disbursement of
children.text:
1. These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India [Disbursement of
Government Pension by Agency Banks (ABs)] Directions, 2026.
2.
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.

children.title: B. Applicability
children.text: B. Applicability

children.title: 3. These Directions shall be applicable to the ABs which are authorized to disburse
children.text:
3. These Directions shall be applicable to the ABs which are authorized to disburse
Central Government (CG) and State Government (SG) pension.

children.title: C. Definitions
children.text: C. Definitions

children.title: 4. In this Master Direction, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
children.text:
4. In this Master Direction, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'ABs' means all Public Sector Banks (PSBs), scheduled Private Sector Banks
(PVBs), scheduled Payments Banks (PBs) and scheduled Small Finance Banks
(SFBs) appointed by RBI under Section 45 of the RBI, Act, 1934, by mutual
agreement, to carry out Government Banking business of CG / SGs.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(2) 'Jeevan Pramaan' means a biometric enabled digital service for pensioners of CG,
SG or any other Government organization.
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have been
assigned to them under the applicable Acts, Rules / Regulations made thereunder, or any
statutory modification or re-enactment thereto or as used in commercial parlance, as the
case may be.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter II - Implementation of Government Orders
hierarchy.text: Chapter II - Implementation of Government Orders

children.title: A. Government Orders on Dearness Relief (DR), etc., available on its website
children.text: A. Government Orders on Dearness Relief (DR), etc., available on its website

children.title: 5. In order to obviate the time lag between issue of DR Orders and payment of DR to
children.text:
5. In order to obviate the time lag between issue of DR Orders and payment of DR to
the beneficiary, and to render expeditious service to senior citizens, the procedure of
forwarding related Government Orders in respect of DR, etc., through RBI to ABs had
been discontinued. ABs shall, therefore, act expeditiously on the basis of copies of
Government Orders provided by Government to them through post, fax, e-mail, etc.,
and/or by regularly accessing the Government websites.

children.title: B. Prompt implementation of Government's Orders by ABs
children.text: B. Prompt implementation of Government's Orders by ABs

children.title: 6. All ABs shall scrupulously follow all the orders contained in various notifications of
children.text:
6. All ABs shall scrupulously follow all the orders contained in various notifications of
Government (Central as well as States) and take necessary action immediately without
waiting for any further instructions from RBI.

children.title: C. Timing of pension disbursement by ABs
children.text: C. Timing of pension disbursement by ABs

children.title: 7. The ABs shall credit the pension amount in the accounts of the pensioners based on
children.text:
7. The ABs shall credit the pension amount in the accounts of the pensioners based on
the orders given by respective Pension Sanctioning Authorities (PSAs).

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter III - Recovery and Refund of Excess Pension Payment
hierarchy.text:
Chapter III - Recovery and Refund of Excess Pension Payment
The banks may ensure that no recovery of excess amount from the pension of a
Government employee shall be effected without the pensioner's knowledge and consent,
or without the issuance of a prior notice. Further, any such recovery must strictly conform
to the applicable service rules and the principles discussed hereinabove.
The banks may take a Letter of Undertaking from the pensioner to the effect that in case
any excess pension is credited to the account of the pensioner, the pensioner is bound to
refund the same to the bank upon receipt of a suitable notice from the bank.

children.title: A. Agency Bank-Attributed Errors
children.text: A. Agency Bank-Attributed Errors

children.title: 8. (i) Whenever excess/wrongful pension payment is made to a pensioner due to bank-
children.text:
8. (i) Whenever excess/wrongful pension payment is made to a pensioner due to bank-
attributed errors such as clerical errors, arithmetical miscalculations or incorrect
application of relevant instructions, the entire amount thereof should be credited to the
Government account in lumpsum immediately on detection.

children.title: (ii) The broad procedure that the banks are required to follow for recovery of such
children.text:
(ii) The broad procedure that the banks are required to follow for recovery of such
excess /wrongful pension payments made to the pensioner, is given in Annex III.

children.title: (iii) The banks shall put in place a Board-approved policy on recovery of
children.text:
(iii) The banks shall put in place a Board-approved policy on recovery of
excess/wrongful pension payment made to the pensioners (including cut-off period
beyond which no recovery shall be made), issue appropriate operating
procedure/instructions, institute robust monitoring mechanism, and ensure their strict
compliance).

children.title: B. Government-Attributed Errors
children.text: B. Government-Attributed Errors

children.title: 9. Whenever such excess/wrongful pension payment is made due to Government
children.text:
9. Whenever such excess/wrongful pension payment is made due to Government
attributed errors, the banks shall make recoveries based on the instructions received from
the Government, presuming that the latter have complied with all the requirements
envisaged by law, including court orders. However, if recovery is to be made from the
account balance of a pensioner (as distinguished from the amount payable as pension in
a month), express authorization from the customer should be made available to the bank
and the same should be kept in its records. If doubts arise regarding the instructions
issued by the Government, the banks shall take them up with the respective Government,
without reference to the Reserve Bank of India.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter IV - Withdrawal of pension by old/ sick/ disabled/ incapacitated pensioners
hierarchy.text: Chapter IV - Withdrawal of pension by old/ sick/ disabled/ incapacitated pensioners

children.title: A. Guidelines on withdrawal of pension
children.text: A. Guidelines on withdrawal of pension

children.title: 8. In order to take care of problems/ difficulties faced by sick and disabled pensioners
children.text:
8. In order to take care of problems/ difficulties faced by sick and disabled pensioners
in withdrawal of pension / family pension, ABs shall categorize such pensioners as under:

children.title: (a) Pensioner who is too ill to sign a cheque / unable to be physically present in the
children.text:
(a) Pensioner who is too ill to sign a cheque / unable to be physically present in the
branch but can put his/her thumb impression on the cheque/withdrawal form.

children.title: (b) Pensioner who is not only unable to be physically present in the branch but also
children.text:
(b) Pensioner who is not only unable to be physically present in the branch but also
not able to put his/ her thumb impression on the cheque/ withdrawal form due to
certain physical defect / incapacity.

children.title: 9. With a view to enabling such old/ sick/ incapacitated pensioners to operate their
children.text:
9. With a view to enabling such old/ sick/ incapacitated pensioners to operate their
accounts, ABs shall follow the procedure as under:

children.title: (a) Wherever thumb or toe impression of the old/ sick pensioner is obtained, it
children.text:
(a) Wherever thumb or toe impression of the old/ sick pensioner is obtained, it
should be identified by two independent witnesses known to the bank, one of
whom should be a responsible bank official

children.title: (b) Where the pensioner cannot even put his/ her thumb/ toe impression and also
children.text:
(b) Where the pensioner cannot even put his/ her thumb/ toe impression and also
is unable to be physically present in the bank, a mark can be obtained on the
cheque/ withdrawal form, which should be identified by two independent
witnesses, one of whom should be a responsible bank official

children.title: (c) The pensioner may also be asked to indicate to the bank as to who would
children.text:
(c) The pensioner may also be asked to indicate to the bank as to who would
withdraw the amount from the bank on the basis of cheque / withdrawal form as
obtained above and that person should be identified by two independent
witnesses. The person who would be actually drawing the money from the bank
should be asked to furnish his signature to the bank

children.title: (d) In case the pensioner cannot sign due to loss of both hands, reference may be
children.text:
(d) In case the pensioner cannot sign due to loss of both hands, reference may be
drawn to the Supreme Court judgement reported in AIR 1950 - Supreme Court,
265, that there must be physical contact between the person who is to sign and
the signature can be by means of a mark. This mark can be placed by the person
in any manner. It could be the toe impression, as suggested. It can be by means
of mark which anybody can put on behalf of the person who has to sign, the

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
mark being put by an instrument which has had a physical contact with the
person who has to sign.
The responsible bank official referred to in Para 11(a) and 11(b), should be from the same
bank, preferably from the same branch, where the pensioner is having his/ her pension
account.

children.title: B. Display of instructions in bank branches
children.text: B. Display of instructions in bank branches

children.title: 10. The ABs' branches should display the instructions issued in this regard on their notice
children.text:
10. The ABs' branches should display the instructions issued in this regard on their notice
board so that sick and disabled pensioners could make full use of these facilities. ABs
shall strictly implement the instructions issued by RBI regarding the facilities to be
provided to the sick and disabled persons, sensitise staff members in the matter, and
refer to the Frequently Asked Question (FAQs) on 'Payment of Pension to Government
Pensioners' hosted on our website (www.rbi.org.in) in case of any doubt.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter V - Reimbursement of pension payments
hierarchy.text: Chapter V - Reimbursement of pension payments

children.title: 11. Link branches of ABs shall submit reimbursement claims to Central Accounts Section
children.text:
11. Link branches of ABs shall submit reimbursement claims to Central Accounts Section
(CAS), Nagpur, RBI / Government Banking Divisions (GBDs) of respective Regional
Offices (ROs) for CG / SG pension payments, respectively.

children.title: 12. Single Window System (SWS) was introduced to facilitate prompt settlement of
children.text:
12. Single Window System (SWS) was introduced to facilitate prompt settlement of
reimbursement claims and reconciliation. The underlying objective is to make each
pension paying bank responsible in its own right to effect settlement without the
intervention of RBI offices, in the process, eliminating cause of delay in reimbursement
claims.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: A. Issuance of Acknowledgement
children.text: A. Issuance of Acknowledgement

children.title: 13. In order to alleviate the hardships faced by pensioners, ABs shall mandatorily issue
children.text:
13. In order to alleviate the hardships faced by pensioners, ABs shall mandatorily issue
duly signed acknowledgements against receipt of life certificate. ABs shall also consider
updating the receipt of life certificates in their Core Banking Solution (CBS) and issue a
system-generated acknowledgement which would serve the twin purpose of
acknowledgement as well as real-time-updation of records. ABs shall provide digital
acknowledgment in respect of digital life certificate submitted by the pensioner.

children.title: B. Jeevan Pramaan
children.text: B. Jeevan Pramaan

children.title: 14. 'Jeevan Pramaan', a digital life certificate launched by Central Pension Accounting
children.text:
14. 'Jeevan Pramaan', a digital life certificate launched by Central Pension Accounting
Office (CPAO), Ministry of Finance (MoF), Government of India (GoI), based on Aadhaar
Biometric Authentication, has simplified the process of submission of life certificate,
facilitating accuracy and timeliness in disbursal of pensions. ABs shall refer to the orders
contained in Office Memorandum dated November 14, 2014, issued by the CPAO
forwarded to all ABs and SGs / Union Territories (UTs), as amended from time-to-time.
Similar amendments in the pension regulations of various CG Ministries (e.g., Ministry of
Railways, Department of Posts, etc.) shall also be referred. All ABs disbursing
Government pension shall take necessary action to implement the scheme and issue
necessary instructions to all their branches concerned and dealing staff. All ABs shall
work towards creating awareness about 'Jeevan Pramaan' among their pensioner-
customers through their branches, websites and other means. ABs shall also suitably
amend the FAQs on pension payments posted on their respective websites and provide
a link to the website of 'Jeevan Pramaan.'

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: A. Role of branches maintaining pension accounts
children.text: A. Role of branches maintaining pension accounts

children.title: 15. The AB branch that maintains pension accounts and also calculates pension should
children.text:
15. The AB branch that maintains pension accounts and also calculates pension should
continue to be the point of referral for the pensioner.

children.title: B. Sympathetic treatment of pensioners
children.text: B. Sympathetic treatment of pensioners

children.title: 16. All branches maintaining pension accounts should guide and assist the pensioners
children.text:
16. All branches maintaining pension accounts should guide and assist the pensioners
in all their dealings with the AB.

children.title: 17. All ABs disbursing pension shall provide considerate and sympathetic customer
children.text:
17. All ABs disbursing pension shall provide considerate and sympathetic customer
service to the pensioners, especially to those pensioners who are of old age.

children.title: C. Adherence to recommendations of Prabhakar Rao Committee on Customer
children.text:
C. Adherence to recommendations of Prabhakar Rao Committee on Customer
Service

children.title: 18. All ABs shall issue instructions to their dealing branches to adhere to the
children.text:
18. All ABs shall issue instructions to their dealing branches to adhere to the
recommendations of the Prabhakar Rao Committee relating to pension payments. A
checklist shall be provided to the inspecting officers/ auditors, which shall, at a minimum,
include the illustrative items given in Annex 1. ABs shall also instruct their internal
auditors/ inspectors to comment on the quality of customer service offered to the
pensioners in their reports, which should be made available to RBI's inspecting officers,
as and when they visit the branches.

children.title: D. Appointment of Nodal Officers (NOs)
children.text: D. Appointment of Nodal Officers (NOs)

children.title: 19. To provide hassle free service to the pensioners, there should be a forum for regular
children.text:
19. To provide hassle free service to the pensioners, there should be a forum for regular
interaction and settlement of grievances. Accordingly, ABs should appoint one/two NOs
at each Region/Zone for monitoring the resolution of grievances of pensioners on
regular basis and the General Manager (GM) / Chief General Manager (CGM)

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
concerned should review the position at monthly intervals.

children.title: 20. At locations outside the Centralised Pension Processing Centre (CPPCs), there
children.text:
20. At locations outside the Centralised Pension Processing Centre (CPPCs), there
should be designated NOs for pension related complaints who should be (a) easily
accessible to pensioners, and (b) they should hold regular meetings at different
locations in their jurisdiction on the lines of Pension Adalat. Each AB should establish
toll-free dedicated pension line manned by trained persons with access to the database
to answer queries, note down and redress complaints.

children.title: E. Continuation of 'either or survivor' pension account after death of a pensioner
children.text: E. Continuation of 'either or survivor' pension account after death of a pensioner

children.title: 21. ABs shall not insist on opening of a new account for credit of family pension after
children.text:
21. ABs shall not insist on opening of a new account for credit of family pension after
death of the pensioner, if the spouse (family pensioner) opts for an existing joint account
for credit of family pension.

children.title: F. Recording of Pension Payment Order (PPO) Numbers on Passbook
children.text: F. Recording of Pension Payment Order (PPO) Numbers on Passbook

children.title: 22. All ABs shall record the PPO numbers in all the pension passbooks of
children.text:
22. All ABs shall record the PPO numbers in all the pension passbooks of
pensioners/family pensioners in order to alleviate the difficulties reported by pensioners/
family pensioners to get duplicate PPO in the instances of (a) missing original PPO, (b)
transfer of pension account from one bank/ branch to another bank/ branch, and (c)
commencement of family pension to spouse or dependent children after the death of
the pensioner, etc.

children.title: G. Issuance of pension slips
children.text: G. Issuance of pension slips

children.title: 23. Pension slips should be issued to the pensioners at (a) commencement of pension,
children.text:
23. Pension slips should be issued to the pensioners at (a) commencement of pension,
and (b) whenever there is a change in the quantum of pension.

children.title: H. Timely credit of pension/ arrears
children.text: H. Timely credit of pension/ arrears

children.title: 24. To ensure timely disbursal of revised pension and arrears, ABs should have a
children.text:
24. To ensure timely disbursal of revised pension and arrears, ABs should have a
mechanism to immediately obtain copies of pension orders from the PPAs so that the
pensioners get benefits announced by Governments in the succeeding month's pension

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
payment itself. ABs shall also place the arithmetic and other details about pension
calculations on the web, to be made available to the pensioners through the net or at
the branches at periodic intervals, as deemed necessary, and to make sufficient
advertisement about such arrangements.

children.title: I. Compensation for delay in credit of pension/ arrears
children.text: I. Compensation for delay in credit of pension/ arrears

children.title: 25. ABs should compensate the pensioner for delay in crediting pension/ arrears thereof
children.text:
25. ABs should compensate the pensioner for delay in crediting pension/ arrears thereof
at a fixed interest rate of 8 per cent per annum for the period of delay after the due date
of payment, if the delay is on account of error on part of the AB. Further, the
compensation shall be credited to the pensioner's account automatically, without any
claim from the pensioner.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: A. Repeal and Saving
children.text: A. Repeal and Saving

children.title: 26. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
children.text:
26. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
guidelines relating to areas covered in these Directions as applicable to ABs and
mentioned in Annex 2 stand repealed. The Directions, instructions, and guidelines
repealed prior to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

children.title: 27. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
children.text:
27. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to be
governed by the provisions thereof. All approvals or acknowledgments granted under
these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions. Further, the
repeal of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially
affect:

children.title: (a) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;
children.text: (a) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;

children.title: (b) any penalty, forfeiture, or punishment incurred in respect of any
children.text:
(b) any penalty, forfeiture, or punishment incurred in respect of any
contravention committed thereunder;

children.title: (c) any investigation, legal proceeding, or remedy in respect of any such right,
children.text:
(c) any investigation, legal proceeding, or remedy in respect of any such right,
privilege, obligation, liability, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment as aforesaid;
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.

children.title: B. Application of Other Laws not barred
children.text: B. Application of Other Laws not barred

children.title: 28. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
children.text:
28. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in
force.

children.title: C. Interpretations
children.text: C. Interpretations

children.title: 29. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
children.text:
29. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
(Sivakumar Bose)
Chief General Manager

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: Annex I
children.text:
Annex I
Checklist relating to Government Business (pension related) for internal/
concurrent audit (Illustrative)
Internal inspections should assess branch performance in servicing pensioner
customers. In this regard, the following, among others, shall be ensured:

children.title: 1. A specific questionnaire covering all aspects of pension payment shall be devised
children.text:
1. A specific questionnaire covering all aspects of pension payment shall be devised
for use during inspection of pension paying branches.

children.title: 2. Inspecting officers shall also, during inspections, call up pensioners at random and
children.text:
2. Inspecting officers shall also, during inspections, call up pensioners at random and
enquire about their satisfaction with pension-related services.

children.title: 3. A detailed checklist relating to pension payments/Government business shall be
children.text:
3. A detailed checklist relating to pension payments/Government business shall be
given by banks to internal auditors/inspectors in order to adhere to the
recommendations of the Prabhakar Rao Committee, constituted by the
Government of India, relating to pension payments/Government business.
These include the following:

children.title: (a) Whether there is delay in payment of pension, revision of pension, revision
children.text:
(a) Whether there is delay in payment of pension, revision of pension, revision
in dearness relief, etc.?

children.title: (b) Whether the branch manager has held structured interaction with a cross
children.text:
(b) Whether the branch manager has held structured interaction with a cross
section of pensioners serviced at the branch, on quarterly basis, where the
number of pensioners of all Governments and departments exceeds a
fixed number, say, 100 or 200?

children.title: (c) Whether nominations have been obtained for all pension accounts and the
children.text:
(c) Whether nominations have been obtained for all pension accounts and the
status is being indicated on the monthly pension slips issued to the
pensioners?

children.title: (d) Whether pension accounts have been converted into joint accounts
children.text:
(d) Whether pension accounts have been converted into joint accounts
wherever applicable?

children.title: (e) Whether the bank branch has an effective complaint redressal mechanism,
children.text:
(e) Whether the bank branch has an effective complaint redressal mechanism,
and the complaints of pensioners are attended promptly, and their
grievances redressed expeditiously?

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (f) Whether the pension is credited to pensioner's account during the last four
children.text:
(f) Whether the pension is credited to pensioner's account during the last four
working days of the month except for the month of March for which pension
is to be credited on or after first working day of April?

children.title: (g) Whether the pension paying branch obtains Life Certificate/ Non-
children.text:
(g) Whether the pension paying branch obtains Life Certificate/ Non-
employment certificate/Employment Certificate from the pensioners in the
month of November every year?

children.title: (h) Whether income tax is being deducted at source from pension payments
children.text:
(h) Whether income tax is being deducted at source from pension payments
wherever applicable?

children.title: (i) Whether paper tokens in acknowledgement of cheques presented are
children.text:
(i) Whether paper tokens in acknowledgement of cheques presented are
invariably given by the tax collecting branches?

children.title: (j) Whether the challans are stamped giving bank's BSR code and Challan
children.text:
(j) Whether the challans are stamped giving bank's BSR code and Challan
Identification Number (CIN) clearly?

children.title: (k) Whether the stamped challans are kept in the custody of bank's staff and
children.text:
(k) Whether the stamped challans are kept in the custody of bank's staff and
handed over to the concerned taxpayer only on production of the paper
token?

children.title: Annex III
children.text:
Annex III
Broad Procedure to be followed by Agency Banks for Recovery of Excess/Wrongful Pension Payment done due to
Agency Bank-Attributed Errors

children.title: 1. As soon as the excess/wrong payment made to a pensioner comes to the notice of the pension disbursing bank, the
children.text:
1. As soon as the excess/wrong payment made to a pensioner comes to the notice of the pension disbursing bank, the
pensioner should be immediately intimated through a written notice. The notice should invariably contain details of
excess/wrong payment made, nature of error, proposed recovery method, and opportunity to make representation to the bank.

children.title: 2. Recovery can be made through one or more of the following methods, in order of preference:
children.text:
2. Recovery can be made through one or more of the following methods, in order of preference:
i.
Available Balance Method: Recovery limited to surplus balance available in the account over a minimum protected
amount.
ii.
Percentage of Pension Method: Deduction capped at an agreed percentage (e.g., 10-25%) of monthly pension.
iii.
Pensioner-Requested Method: Lump-sum or customized instalments as required by the pensioner.

children.title: 3. No recovery shall be initiated after a defined cut-off period unless:
children.text: 3. No recovery shall be initiated after a defined cut-off period unless:

children.title: • Fraud or misrepresentation by the pensioner is established.
children.text: • Fraud or misrepresentation by the pensioner is established.

children.title: • Recovery is mandated by law or court order.
children.text: • Recovery is mandated by law or court order.

children.title: 4. If the excess/wrong payment cannot be recovered from the pensioner due to his / her death or discontinuance of pension, then action has to be taken as per the Letter of Authorization given by the pensioner.
children.text: 4. If the excess/wrong payment cannot be recovered from the pensioner due to his / her death or discontinuance of pension, then action has to be taken as per the Letter of Authorization given by the pensioner.
